response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack engineer with a long career, but his background is almost entirely in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang — the two core requirements of this role, deep Java/Kotlin expertise and Spring ecosystem experience, are essentially absent. Java appears only incidentally (robotic arms at Altom, a brief stint at National Instruments on a C IDE), and there is no mention of Kotlin, Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or JVM internals anywhere in the resume. While his work at National Instruments on NI LabWindows/CVI is tangentially related to IDE tooling, it targets a C environment rather than JVM, and dates to over two decades ago. The role demands someone who lives and breathes enterprise Java/Kotlin tooling, which this candidate clearly does not.
strengths: Genuine IDE/developer-tooling background via NI LabWindows/CVI work and open-source D language tooling (Trial, Fluent-Asserts)
Strong software craftsmanship culture — TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development practiced across multiple roles
Long, varied career demonstrating adaptability and ownership across complex systems at scale
concerns: No demonstrated Java or Kotlin expertise at any meaningful depth; JVM internals knowledge is absent from the resume
Zero experience with Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any enterprise Java framework
Primary languages throughout career are TypeScript/Node.js and DLang — neither aligns with the JVM tooling focus of this role
